_README: Vision-OCR cache. In production this is populated automatically by Anthropic Claude Haiku API calls (~$0.003/page). Each entry is the structured output of the VISION_PROMPT in ocr_backend.py. Cache is keyed by '<packet>_<filename>' or '<filename>'.

highlighted_regions.interpretation: Cleo highlighted column 5 in green — this column is the order-specific re-test data for Olive Nation's WO# 11392 (overlaid on the original CraftMark run). 23.0% moisture and 2560 ppm sulfur in this column MATCH Olive's COA p4 values.

olive_p-11.png.notes: PAGE HAS DUAL-PURPOSE DATA. The page itself is the original CraftMark Bakery SQR for the run that produced the bins now used for Olive's extra-case order. Cleo's GREEN highlight on column 5 marks the Olive-specific re-test column — moisture 23.0%, sulfur 2560 ppm, product 'Single Diced Peaches', WO# 11392 — which MATCHES Olive's COA p4 and Extra-Case SQR p10 values. CraftMark's original-run averages (24.5% / 2240 ppm) are backup-source reference data only.

pedrick_p-13.png.notes: WO 11592 is a NEW WO that pulled apricots from multiple Garry Packing original-source bins (not a single source like other extra-case orders). 50 cases @ 25 lbs total. | NOTE: This page handwrites Sulfur Reading as 3136 ppm; the COA p5 prints 3126 ppm. Real discrepancy on the packet — likely a transcription typo by the QC tech (3136 vs 3126 — a 6/2 swap is a classic handwriting confusion).
